author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

On the words of the Gospel, Matt. viii. 8, ‘I am not worthy that thou shouldest come under my roof,’ etc., and of the words of the apostle, 1 Cor. viii. 10, ‘For if a man see thee who hast knowledge sitting at meat in an idol’s temple,’ etc.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Matt. viii. 8, ‘I am not worthy that thou shouldest come under my roof,’ etc., and of the words of the apostle, 1 Cor. viii. 10, ‘For if a man see thee who hast knowledge sitting at meat in an idol’s temple,’ etc.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We have heard, as the Gospel was being read, the praise of our faith as manifested in humility.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when the Lord Jesus promised that He would go to the Centurion’s house to heal His servant, He answered, “I am not worthy that Thou shouldest come under my roof: but speak the word only, and he shall be healed.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By calling himself unworthy, he showed himself worthy for Christ to come not into his house, but into his heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nor would he have said this with so great faith and humility, had he not borne Him in his heart, of whose coming into his house he was afraid.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it were no great happiness for the Lord Jesus to enter into his house, and yet not to be in his heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this Master of humility both by word and example, sat down even in the house of a certain proud Pharisee, by name Simon; and though He sat down in his house, there was no place in this heart, “where the Son of Man could lay His Head.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For so, as we may understand from the words of the Lord Himself, did He call back from His discipleship a certain proud man, who of his own accord was desirous to go with Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Lord, I will follow Thee whithersoever Thou goest.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And the Lord seeing in his heart what was invisible, said, “Foxes have holes, and the birds of the air have nests, but the Son of Man hath not where to lay His Head.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That is, in thee, guile like the fox doth dwell, and pride as the birds of heaven.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the Son of Man simple as opposed to guile, lowly as opposed to pride, hath not where to lay His Head; and this very laying, not the raising up of the head, teaches humility.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore doth He call back this one who was desirous to go, and another who refused He draweth onward.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For in the same place He saith to a certain man, “Follow Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And he said, “I will follow Thee, Lord, but let me first go and bury my father.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: His excuse was indeed a dutiful one: and therefore was he the more worthy to have his excuse removed, and his calling confirmed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What he wished to do was an act of dutifulness; but the Master taught him what he ought to prefer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He wished him to be a preacher of the living word, to make others live.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But there were others by whom that first necessary office might be fulfilled.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Let the dead,” He saith, “bury their dead.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When unbelievers bury a dead body, the dead bury the dead.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The body of the one hath lost its soul, the soul of the others hath lost God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For as the soul is the life of the body; so is God the life of the soul.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As the body expires when it loses the soul, so doth the soul expire when it loses God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The loss of God is the death of the soul: the loss of the soul the death of the body.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The death of the body is necessary; the death of the soul voluntary.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Lord then sat down in the house of a certain proud Pharisee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He was in his house, as I have said, and was not in his heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But into this centurion’s house He entered not, yet He possessed his heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Zacchæus again received the Lord both in house and heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet the centurion’s faith is praised for its humility.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he said, “I am not worthy that Thou shouldest come under my roof;” and the Lord said, “Verily I say unto you, I have not found so great faith, no, not in Israel;” according to the flesh, that is.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he too was an Israelite undoubtedly according to the spirit.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Lord had come to fleshly Israel, that is, to the Jews, there to seek first for the lost sheep, among this people, and of this people also He had assumed His Body.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I have not found there so great faith,” He saith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We can but measure the faith of men, as men can judge of it; but He who saw the inward parts, He whom no man can deceive, gave His testimony to this man’s heart, hearing words of lowliness, and pronouncing a sentence of healing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But whence did he get such confidence?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I also,” saith he, “am a man set under authority, having soldiers under me: and I say to this man, Go, and he goeth; and to another, Come, and he cometh: and to my servant, Do this, and he doeth it.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I am an authority to certain who are placed under me, being myself placed under a certain authority above me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then I a man under authority have the power of commanding, what power must Thou have, whom all powers serve?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now this man was of the Gentiles, for he was a centurion.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: At that time the Jewish nation had soldiers of the Roman empire among them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There he was engaged in a military life, according to the extent of a centurion’s authority, both under authority himself, and having authority over others; as a subject obedient, ruling others who were under him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the Lord (and mark this especially, Beloved, as need there is you should), though He was among the Jewish people only, even now announced beforehand that the Church should be in the whole world, for the establishment of which He would send Apostles; Himself not seen, yet believed on by the Gentiles: by the Jews seen, and put to death.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For as the Lord did not in body enter into this man’s house, and still, though in body absent, yet present in majesty, healed his faith, and his house; so the same Lord also was in body among the Jewish people only: among the other nations He was neither born of a Virgin, nor suffered, nor walked, nor endured His human sufferings, nor wrought His divine miracles.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: None of all this took place in the rest of the nations, and yet was that fulfilled which was spoken of Him, “A people whom I have not known, hath served Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And how if it did not know Him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Hath obeyed Me by the hearing of the ear.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Jewish nation knew, and crucified Him; the whole world besides heard and believed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This absence, so to say, of His body, and presence of His power among all nations, He signified also in the instance of that woman who had touched the edge of His garment, when He asketh, saying, “Who touched Me?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He asketh, as though He were absent; as though present, He healeth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The multitude,” say the disciples, “press Thee, and sayest Thou, Who touched Me?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For as if He were so walking as not to be touched by anybody at all, He said, “Who touched Me?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And they answer, “The multitude press Thee.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And the Lord would seem to say, I am asking for one who touched, not for one who pressed Me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In this case also is His Body now, that is, His Church.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The faith of the few “touches” it, the throng of the many “press” it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For ye have heard, as being her children, that Christ’s Body is the Church, and if ye will, ye yourselves are so.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This the Apostle says in many places, “For His body’s sake, which is the Church;” and again, “But ye are the body of Christ, and members in particular.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then we are His body, what His body then suffered in the crowd, that doth His Church suffer now.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is pressed by many, touched by few.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The flesh presses it, faith touches it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lift up therefore your eyes, I beseech you, ye who have wherewithal to see.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For ye have before you something to see.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lift up the eyes of faith, touch but the extreme border of His garment, it will be sufficient for saving health.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See ye how that which ye have heard out of the Gospel was at that time to come is now present.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore, said He, on occasion of the commendation of the Centurion’s faith, as in the flesh an alien, but of the household in heart, “Therefore I say unto you, Many shall come from the east and west.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Not all, but “many;” yet they shall “come from the East and West;” the whole world is denoted by these two parts.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Many shall come from the east and west, and shall sit down with Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob, in the kingdom of heaven; but the children of the kingdom shall be cast out into outer darkness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But the children of the kingdom,” the Jews, namely.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And how “the children of the kingdom”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because they received the Law; to them the Prophets were sent, with them was the temple and the Priesthood; they celebrated the figures of all the things to come.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet of what things they celebrated the figures, they acknowledged not the presence.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And, “Therefore the children of the kingdom,” He saith, shall go into outer darkness, there shall be wailing and gnashing of teeth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so we see the Jews reprobate, and Christians called from the East and West, to the heavenly banquet, to sit down with Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob, where the bread is righteousness, and the cup wisdom.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Consider then, brethren, for of these are ye; ye are of this people, even then foretold, and now exhibited.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yes, verily, ye are of those who have been called from the East and West, to sit down in the kingdom of heaven, not in the temple of idols.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be ye then the Body of Christ, not the pressure of His Body.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye have the border of His garment to touch, that ye may be healed of the issue of blood, that is, of carnal pleasures.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye have, I say, the border of the garment to touch.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Look upon the Apostles as the garment, by the texture of unity clinging closely to the sides of Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Among these Apostles was Paul, as it were the border, the least and last; as he saith himself, “I am the least of the Apostles.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In a garment the last and least thing is the border.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The border is in appearance contemptible, yet is it touched with saving efficacy.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Even to this hour we both hunger and thirst, and are naked and buffeted.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What state so low, so contemptible as this!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Touch then, if thou art suffering from a bloody flux.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There will go power out of Him whose garment it is, and it will heal thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The border was proposed to you just now to be touched, when out of the same Apostle there was read, “For if any one see him which hath knowledge sit at meat in an idol’s temple, shall not the conscience of him who is weak, be emboldened to eat things offered to idols?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And through thy knowledge shall thy weak brother perish, for whom Christ died!”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How think ye may men be deceived by idols, which they suppose are honoured by Christians?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A man may say, “God knows my heart.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yes, but thy brother did not know thy heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou art weak, beware of a still greater weakness; if thou art strong, have a care of thy brother’s weakness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They who see what you do, are emboldened to do more, so as to desire not only to eat, but also to sacrifice there.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And lo, “Through thy knowledge the weak brother perisheth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear then, my brother; if thou didst disregard the weak, wouldest thou disregard a brother also?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What if so thou sin against Christ Himself?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For attend to what thou canst not by any means disregard.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But,” saith he, “when ye sin so against the brethren, and wound their weak conscience, ye sin against Christ.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let them who disregard these words, go now, and sit at meat in the idol’s temple; will they not be of those who press, and do not touch?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And when they have been at meat in the idol’s temple, let them come and fill the Church; not to receive saving health, but to make a pressure there.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But thou wilt say, I am afraid lest I offend those above me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By all means be afraid of offending them, and so thou wilt not offend God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thou who art afraid lest thou offend those above thee, see whether there be not One above him whom thou art afraid of offending.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By all means then be loth to offend those above thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is an established rule with thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But then is it not plain, that he must on no account be offended, who is above all others?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Run over now the list of those above thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: First are thy father and mother, if they are educating thee aright; if they are bringing thee up for Christ; they are to be heard in all things, they must be obeyed in every command; let them enjoin nothing against one above themselves, and so let them be obeyed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And who, thou wilt say, is above him who begat me?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He who created thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For man begets, but God creates.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How it is that man begets, he does not know; and what he shall beget, he does not know.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But He who saw thee that He might make thee, before that he whom He made existed, is surely above thy father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thy country again should be above thy very parents; so that whereinsoever thy parents enjoin aught against thy country, they are not to be listened to.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And whatsoever thy country enjoin against God, it is not to be listened to.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if thou wilt be healed, if after the issue of blood, if after twelve years’ continuance in that disease, if after having spent thine all upon physicians, and not having received health, thou dost wish at length to be made whole; O woman, whom I am addressing as a figure of the Church, thy father enjoineth thee this, and thy people that.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But thy Lord saith to thee, “Forget thine own people, and thy father’s house.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what good? for what advantage? with what useful result?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Because the King hath desired thy beauty.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He hath desired what He made, since when deformed He loved thee, that He might make thee beautiful.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thee unbelieving, and deformed, He shed His Blood, and He made thee faithful and beauteous, He hath loved His own gifts in thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what didst thou bring to thy spouse?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What didst thou receive for dowry from thy former father, and former people?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Was it not the excesses and the rags of sins?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thy rags He cast away, thy robe impure He tore asunder.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He pitied thee that He might adorn thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He adorned thee, that He might love thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What need of more, Brethren.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye are Christians, and have heard, that “If ye sin against the brethren, and wound their weak conscience, ye sin against Christ.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do not disregard it, if ye would not be wiped out of the book of life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How long shall I go about to speak in bright and pleasing terms to you, what my grief forceth me to speak in some sort, and will not suffer me to keep secret?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whosoever they are who are minded to disregard these things, and sin against Christ, let them only consider what they are doing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We wish the rest of the Heathen to be gathered in; and ye are stones in their way: they have a wish to come; they stumble, and so return.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For they say in their hearts, Why should we leave the gods whom the very Christians worship as we do?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God forbid, thou wilt say, that I should worship the gods of the Gentiles.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I know, I understand, I believe thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what account art thou making of the consciences of the weak which thou art wounding?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What account art thou making of their price, if thou disregard the purchase?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Consider for how great a price was the purchase made.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Through thy knowledge,” saith the Apostle, “shall the weak brother perish;” that knowledge which thou professest to have, in that thou knowest that an idol is nothing, and that in thy mind thou art thinking only of God, and so sittest down in the idol’s temple.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In this knowledge the weak brother perisheth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And lest thou shouldest pay no regard to the weak brother, he added, “for whom Christ died.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou wouldest disregard him, yet consider his Price, and weigh the whole world in the balance with the Blood of Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And lest thou shouldest still think that thou art sinning against a weak brother, and so esteem it after that he had heard that he was “Peter,” a trivial fault, and of small account, he saith, “Ye sin against Christ.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For men are in the habit of saying, I sin against man; am I sinning against God?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Deny then that Christ is God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Dost thou dare deny that Christ is God?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hast thou learned this other doctrine, when thou didst sit at meat in the idol’s temple?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The school of Christ doth not admit that doctrine.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I ask; Where learnedst thou that Christ is not God?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Pagans are wont to say so.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Seest thou what bad associations do?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Seest thou, “that evil communications corrupt good manners?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There thou canst not speak of the Gospel, and thou dost hear others talking of idols.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There thou losest the truth that Christ is God; and what thou dost drink in there, thou vomitest out in the Church.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It may be thou art bold enough to speak here; bold enough to mutter among the crowds; “Was not then Christ a man?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Was He not crucified?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This hast thou learned of the Pagans.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast lost thy soul’s health, thou hast not touched the border.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: On this point then touch again the border, and receive health.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As I taught thee to touch it in this that is written, “Whoso seeth a brother sit at meat in the idol’s temple;” touch it also concerning the Divinity of Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The same border said of the Jews, “Whose are the fathers, and of whom as concerning the flesh Christ came, who is over all, God blessed for ever.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Behold, against Whom, even the Very God, thou dost sin, when thou sittest down with false gods.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is no god, you will say; because it is the tutelary genius of Carthage.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As though if it were Mars or Mercury, it would be a god.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But consider in what light it is esteemed by them; not what it is in itself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For I know also as well as thou, that it is but a stone.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If this “genius” be any ornament, let the citizens of Carthage live well; and they themselves will be this “genius” of Carthage.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if the “genius” be a devil, ye have heard in that same Scripture, “The things which the Gentiles sacrifice, they sacrifice to devils, and not to God; and I would not that ye should have fellowship with devils.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We know well that it is no God; would that they knew it too! but because of those weak ones who do not know it, their conscience ought not to be wounded.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is this that the Apostle warns us of.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For that they regard that statue as something divine, and take it for a god, the altar is witness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What does the altar there, if it be not accounted a god?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let no one tell me; it is no deity, it is no God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I have said already, “Would that they only knew this, as we all do.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But how they regard it, for what they take it, and what they do about it, that altar is witness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is convincing against the intentions of all who worship there, grant that it may not be convincing also against those who sit at meat with them!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yes, let not Christians press the Church, if the Pagans do.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: She is the Body of Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Were we not saying, that the Body of Christ was pressed, and not touched.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He endured those who pressed Him; and was looking out for those who “touched” Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And, Brethren, I would that if the Body of Christ be pressed by Pagans, by whom it is wont to be pressed; that at least Christians would not press the Body of Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Brethren, it is my business to speak to you, my business it is to speak to Christians; “For what have I to do to judge them that are without?” the Apostle himself saith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Them we address in another way, as being weak.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: With them we must deal softly, that they may hear the truth; in you the corruption must be cut out.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If ye ask whereby the Pagans are to be gained over, whereby they are to be illuminated, and called to salvation; forsake their solemnities, forsake their trifling shows; and then if they do not consent to our truth, let them blush at their own scantiness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If he who is over thee be a good man, he is thy nourisher; if a bad man, he is thy tempter.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Receive the nourishment in the one case with gladness, and in the temptation show thyself approved.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be thou gold.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Regard this world as the furnace of the goldsmith; in one narrow place are there things, gold, chaff, fire.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To the two former the fire is applied, the chaff is burned, and the gold purified.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A man has yielded to threats, and been led away to the idol’s temple: Alas!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I bewail the chaff; I see the ashes.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Another has not yet yielded to threats nor terrors; has been brought before the judge, and stood firm in his confession, and has not bent down to the idol image: what does the flame with him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Does it not purify the gold?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Stand, fast then, Brethren, in the Lord; greater in power, is He who hath called you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be not afraid of the threats of the ungodly.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Bear with your enemies; in them ye have those for whom ye may pray; let them by no means terrify you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is saving health, draw out in this feast here from this source; here drink that wherewith ye may be satisfied, and not in those other feasts, that only whereby ye may be maddened.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Stand fast in the Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye are silver, ye shall be gold.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This similitude is not our own, it is out of Holy Scripture.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye have read and heard, “As gold in the furnace hath He tried them, and received them as a burnt-offering.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See what ye shall be among the treasures of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be ye rich as touching God, not as if to make Him rich, but as to become rich from Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let Him replenish you; admit nought else into your heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do we lift up ourselves unto pride, or tell you to be despisers against the powers ordained?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do ye again who are sick on this point, touch also that border of the garment?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Apostle himself saith, “Let every soul be subject unto the higher powers, for there is no power but of God, the powers that be are ordained of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He then who resisteth the power, resisteth the ordinance of God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what if it enjoin what thou oughtest not to do?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In this case by all means disregard the power through fear of Power.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Consider these several grades of human powers.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If the magistrate enjoin anything, must it not be done?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet if his order be in opposition to the Proconsul, thou dost not surely despise the power, but choosest to obey a greater power.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nor in this case ought the less to be angry, if the greater be preferred.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Again, if the Proconsul himself enjoin anything, and the Emperor another thing, is there any doubt, that disregarding the former, we ought to obey the latter?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then if the Emperor enjoin one thing, and God another, what judge ye?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Pay me tribute, submit thyself to my allegiance.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Right, but not in an idol’s temple.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In an idol’s temple He forbids it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who forbids it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A greater Power.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Pardon me then: thou threatenest a prison, He threateneth hell.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Here must thou at once take to thee thy “faith as a shield, whereby thou mayest be able to quench all the fiery darts of the enemy.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But one of these powers is plotting, and contriving evil designs against thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Well: he is but sharpening the razor wherewith to shave the hair, but not to cut the head.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye have but just now heard this that I have said in the Psalm, “Thou hast worked deceit like a sharp razor.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why did He compare the deceit of a wicked man in power to a razor?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because it does not reach, save to our superfluous parts.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As hairs on our body seem as it were superfluous, and are shaven off without any loss of the flesh; so whatsoever an angry man in power can take from thee, count only among thy superfluities.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He takes away thy poverty; can he take away thy wealth?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thy poverty is thy wealth in thy heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thy superfluous things only hath he power to take away, these only hath he power to injure, even though he had license given him so far as to hurt the body.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yea even this life itself to those whose thoughts are of another life, this present life, I say, may be reckoned among the things superfluous.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For so the Martyrs have despised it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They did not lose life, but they gained Life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be sure, Brethren, that enemies have no power against the faithful, except so far as it profiteth them to be tempted and proved.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Of this be sure, Brethren, let no one say ought against it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Cast all your care upon the Lord, throw yourselves wholly and entirely upon Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He will not withdraw Himself that ye should fall.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He who created us, hath given us security touching our very hairs.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Verily I say unto you, even the hairs of your head are all numbered.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Our hairs are numbered by God; how much more is our conduct known to Him to whom our hairs are thus known?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See then, how that God doth not disregard our least things.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if He disregarded them, He would not create them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He verily both created our hairs, and still taketh count of them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But thou wilt say, though they are preserved at present, perhaps they will perish.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: On this point also hear His word, “Verily I say unto you, there shall not an hair of your head perish.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why art thou afraid of man, O man, whose place is in the Bosom of God?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Fall not out of His Bosom; whatsoever thou shall suffer there, will avail to thy salvation, not to thy destruction.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Martyrs have endured the tearing of their limbs, and shall Christians fear the injuries of Christian times?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He who would do thee an injury now, can only do it in fear.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He does not say openly, come to the idol-feast; he does not say openly, come to my altars, and banquet there.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And if he should say so, and thou wast to refuse, let him make a complaint of it, let him bring it as an accusation and charge against thee: “He would not come to my altars, he would not come to my temple, where I worship.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let him say this.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He does not dare; but in his guile he contrives another attack.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Make ready thy hair; he is sharpening the razor; he is about to take off thy superfluous things, to shave what thou must soon leave behind thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let him take off what shall endure, if he can.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This powerful enemy, what has he taken away? what great thing has he taken away?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That which a thief or housebreaker could take: in his utmost rage, he can but take what a robber can.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Even if he should have license given him to the slaying of the very body, what does he take away, but what the robber can take?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I did him too much honour, when I said, “a robber.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For be the robber who and what he may, he is a man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He takes from thee what a fever, or an adder, or a poisonous mushroom can take.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Here lies the whole power of the rage of men, to do what a mushroom can!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Men eat a poisonous mushroom, and they die.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo! in what frail estate is the life of man; which sooner or later thou must abandon; do not struggle then in such wise for it, as that thou shouldest be abandoned thyself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Christ is our Life; think then of Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He came to suffer, but also to be glorified; to be despised, but to be exalted also; to die; but also to rise again.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If the labour alarm thee, see its reward.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why dost thou wish to arrive by softness at that to which nothing but hard labour can lead?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now thou art afraid, lest thou shouldest lose thy money; because thou earnest thy money with great labour.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou didst not attain to thy money, which thou must some time or other lose, at all events when thou diest, without labour, wouldest thou desire without labour to attain to the Life eternal?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let that be of higher value in thine eyes, to which after all thy labours thou shalt in such sort attain as never more to lose it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If this money, to which thou hast attained after all thy labours on such condition as that thou must some time lose it, be of high value with thee; how much more ought we to long after those things which are everlasting!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Give no credit to their words, neither be afraid of them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They say that we are enemies of their idols.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: May God so grant, and give all into our power, as He hath already given us that which we have broken down.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this I say, Beloved, that ye may not attempt to do it, when it is not lawfully in your power to do it; for it is the way of ill-regulated men, and the mad Circumcelliones, both to be violent when they have no power, and to be ever eager in their wishes to die without a cause.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye heard what we read to you, all of you who were present in the Mappalia.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “When the land shall have been given into your power” (he saith first, “into your power,” and so enjoined what was to be done); “then,” saith he, “ye shall destroy their altars, and break in pieces their groves, and hew down all their images.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When we shall have got the power, do this.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When the power has not been given us, we do not do it; when it is given, we do not neglect it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Many Pagans have these abominations on their own estates; do we go and break them in pieces?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No, for our first efforts are that the idols in their hearts should be broken down.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When they too are made Christians themselves, they either invite us to so good a work, or anticipate us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: At present we must pray for them, not be angry with them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If very painful feelings excite us, it is rather against Christians, it is against our brethren, who will enter into the Church in such a mind, as to have their body there, and their heart anywhere else.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The whole ought to be within.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If that which man seeth is within, why is that which God seeth without?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now ye may know, Dearly Beloved, that these unite their murmurings with Heretics and with Jews.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Heretics, Jews, and Heathens have made a unity against Unity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because it has happened, that in some places the Jews have received chastisement because of their wickednesses; they charge and suspect us, or pretend, that we are always seeking the like treatment for them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Again, because it has happened that the heretics in some places have suffered the penalty of the laws for the impiety and fury of their deeds of violence; they say immediately that we are seeking by every means some harm for their destruction.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Again, because it has been resolved that laws should be passed against the Heathen, yea for them rather, if they were only wise.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: (For as when silly boys are playing with the mud, and dirtying their hands, the strict master comes, shakes the mud out of their hands, and holds out their book; so has it pleased God by the hands of princes His subjects to alarm their childish, foolish hearts, that they may throw away the dirt from their hands, and set about something useful.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what is this something useful with the hands, but, “Break thy bread to the hungry, and bring the houseless poor into thy house”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But nevertheless these children escape from their master’s sight, and return stealthily to their mud, and when they are discovered they hide their hands that they may not be seen.)

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because then it has so pleased God, they think that we are looking out for the idols everywhere, and that we break them down in all places where we have discovered them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Are there not places before our very eyes in which they are?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Or are we indeed ignorant where they are?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And yet we do not break them down, because God has not given them into our power.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When does God give them into our power?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When the masters of these things shall become Christians.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The master of a certain place has just lately wished this to be done.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If he had not been minded to give the place itself to the Church, and only had given orders that there should be no idols on his property; I think that it ought to have been executed with the greatest devotion, that the soul of the absent Christian brother, who wishes on his land to return thanks to God, and would not that there should be anything there to God’s dishonour, might be assisted by his fellow-Christians.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Added to this, that in this case he gave the place itself to the Church.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And shall there be idols in the Church’s estate?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Brethren, see then what it is that displeases the Heathens.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is but a little matter with them that we do not take them away from their estates, that we do not break them down: they would have them kept up even in our own places.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We preach against idols, we take them away from the hearts of men; we are persecutors of idols; we openly profess it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Are we then to be the preservers of them?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I do not touch them when I have not the power; I do not touch them when the lord of the property complains of it; but when he wishes it to be done, and gives thanks for it, I should incur guilt if I did it not.
